
## Potential as "intrinsic energy of inertia"


{{< include C:/Users/erine/Desktop/Physics-Explorations/GitHub-Physics/PhysEcon/Content/EMfield.qmd >}}


{{< include Maxwell_slow.qmd >}}


## Potential

$\Rightarrow$ 
Wave equation that determines Potential:
$$\begin{split} &
\Delta \cdot U - \frac{1}{c^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} U = J
\end{split}$${#eq-potignit}

$\Rightarrow$
Potential caused by "ignition J" (inhomgenity) from source at rest, v = 0:
$$\begin{split} &
U = -\frac{1}{4\pi} \int \frac{J}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\end{split}$${#eq-potelec}

## elec. Potential structure:
$J = -\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \varrho$
$$\begin{split} &
U_e \coloneqq \Phi 
\newline & 
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. charge dens.=}\varrho}{=}
\frac{1}{4\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \int \frac{\varrho}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline & 
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} Q = 
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} It
\end{split}$${#eq-potelec}

## magn. Potential structure: 
$J = -\mu_0 j$
$$\begin{split} &
U_m \coloneqq \boldsymbol{A} 
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. current dens.=}j}{=}
\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \int \frac{j}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \mu_0 I =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} I =
\frac{1}{c^2} \frac{1}{t} U_e 
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{source in uniformal movement,} v = const. \neq 0}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \mu_0 Qv
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} vQ
\newline & 
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Itv = 
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Ir
\end{split}$${#eq-potmagn}

\

## Electric endogen energy 

- Bond Energy
- Binding Energy

$E_{pot} = mc^2$

$$\begin{split} &
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
q = ne = \textnormal{charge} \qquad
n \in \mathbb{N}
\end{split}$${#eq-echarge}

$$\begin{split} &
\textnormal{Potential} \qquad
U = 
\frac{E_{pot}}{q} = 
\frac{\left( mc^2 \right)^2}{ne} = 
\frac{m}{q} m c^4 = 
\kappa m c^4
\end{split}$${#eq-potential-1}


## Electric induced energy (exogenous excitation)

- Ionisation Energy
- Exitation Energy

$E_{kin} = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \> \hat{=} \> \left( pc \right)^2 \> \hat{=} \> h\nu$

$E_{kin}$ Energy density electric:
$$\begin{split} &
w_e = \frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E}^2
\end{split}$${#eq-energydensity-e}

$E_{kin}$ Energy density magnetic:
$$\begin{split} &
w_m = \frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B}^2
\end{split}$${#eq-energydensity-m}

$E_{kin}$ Electric Energy:
$$\begin{split} &
W_e = \frac{1}{2} CU^2
\end{split}$${#eq-electricenergy}

$E_{kin}$ Magnetic Energy:
$$\begin{split} &
W_m = \frac{1}{2} LI^2
\end{split}$${#eq-magneticenergy}

Power:
$$\begin{split} &
P = VI = (U_1 - U_2) \frac{Q}{t} = 
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{t} = \frac{W}{t} = \frac{Fr}{t} = Fv 
\end{split}$${#eq-power}

### Conductor (charge conductive element for energy transfer) condition:
$$\begin{split} & 
\sigma \cdot t \gg \varepsilon_0
\end{split}$${#eq-conductor}

### Slow changing current (quasi-stationary, low frequencies) condition:
$$\begin{split} & 
\frac{v}{c} \ll \frac{1}{c}\frac{\mathscr{E}}{\mathscr{B}} \ll \frac{c}{v}
\end{split}$${#eq-slowcurrent}

## Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
$$\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( we + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} 
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr 
\newline & 
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split}$${#eq-energyconserv-1}  
 
## Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):

$$\begin{split} &
V = 
U_1 - U_2 = 
r \mathscr{E} = 
\dot{\Psi} + R I + \frac{I}{C}t = 
L \dot{I} + R I + \frac{Q}{C}
\end{split}$${#eq-potential-3}

$$
\Delta \cdot \coloneqq \textnormal{ div grad = Laplacian}
$$\linebreak$$  
J = \textnormal{"ignition of inhomogenity,cause"} \quad
j = \textnormal{Current density} \quad
\varrho = \textnormal{Charge density}
$$\linebreak$$  
V = \textnormal{Potentialdifference} \quad  
I = \textnormal{Current} \quad
R = \textnormal{Resistance} \quad
$$\linebreak$$  
L = \textnormal{Inductance} \qquad  
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
Q = c\textnormal{harge (ion)} \qquad
\Psi = LI = \textnormal{mag. Flux}
$$\linebreak$$ 
\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} = \textnormal{Poynting-Vector = radiation intensity}
$$\linebreak$$
D_S = \int \left( \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} \right) \, dr = \int \textnormal{div}(\mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H}) \, dr = \textnormal{dissipation engergy lost from radiation} \approx \textnormal{0 for low frequencies } \omega
$$\linebreak$$
d = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{\omega}} = \textnormal{skin depth (surface) of conductor} \qquad
r = \frac{d}{\sqrt{2i}}\rho = \frac{d}{1+i}\rho = \textnormal{radius (extension) of conductor}
$$\linebreak$$
\rho = kr = \textnormal{"radius of curvature $k$"}
$$\linebreak$$ 
\omega = \dot{\rho} = \frac{v}{r} = 2 \pi \nu = k \lambda \frac{1}{T}  = \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}
\> \hat{=} \>
kc
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} 
\> \hat{=} \>
\sqrt{\frac{g}{r}}
= \textnormal{frequency} 
$$\linebreak$$ 
\mu = \textnormal{permeability of conductor}    \qquad
\sigma = \textnormal{conductivity of conductor (dielectricum)} \qquad
$$

\

## Low Frequency
$$\begin{split} & \omega \ll \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \ll d \iff \rho \ll 1 \end{split}$${#eq-lowfreq-cond}
$$
L \approx const.    \qquad
C \approx const.    \qquad
D_S \approx 0
$$

## High Frequency
$$\begin{split} & \omega \gg \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \gg d \iff \rho \gg 1 \end{split}$${#eq-highfreq-cond}
$$
L(t) \neq const.    \qquad
C(t) \neq const.   \qquad
\textnormal{for Dipol: } D_S \propto \omega^4 \gg 0 
$$

\

## Continuity at Rest: Electic Charge
no Dissipation, no velocity, charge (ion) causes Electric Field


## Continuity at Movement: Magnetic Field
no Dissipation, Velocity constant, moved charge (ion) causes emergence of Magnetic Field


## Continuity at Acceleration: Radiation Energy
no Dissipation, Velocity changing, acceleration of charge (ion) causes emgergence of Radiation


## Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM

$$
2\pi = k \lambda \gg kr_0 \ll 1
$$\linebreak$$
\omega = kc \ll \frac{c}{r_0}     \qquad
\nabla \cdot j = i \omega \varrho     \qquad
i \omega Q = 0
$$



## Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
Refraction index emerges

Dispersion: $k^2 = \varepsilon \mu \omega^2 + i \mu \sigma \omega$



## Near Field

$$
r \ll \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \ll 2\pi
$$

$$
\frac{w_m}{w_e} \sim \frac{ \frac{\vec{p}^2}{\varepsilon_0 r^6} }{ \frac{k^2 \mu_0 c^2 \vec{p}^2}{r^4} } \sim \left( kr \right)^2 \ll 1 
$$

$$
\vec{p} = \textnormal{elec. Dipol}
$$



## Far Field

accelerated movement -> Radiation in Far Field of charge source

$$
r \gg \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \gg 1
\qquad
r \gg kr_0^2 \ll 1
$$

$$
\frac{w_m}{w_e} =
\frac{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 }{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 } = 1
$$


## Dissipation in Vacuum
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter, no Refraction index, Reflection


## Dissipation in Matter
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter with Dielectricum, Refraction merges, Reflection, Absorbtion


\

## Energy gain by excitation:

{{< include Quant_h.qmd >}}

\

$\Rightarrow \textnormal{Ionisation Energy of chemical element } E_i = h\nu = h\frac{\lambda}{c} = \hbar\omega = \frac{h}{k\lambda} \omega$

$\Rightarrow \textnormal{Energy released (gained) } E_{gain} = E_{pot} - E_i = mc^2 - h\nu$
